raw_comment:
1) I noticed that women contact their parents more often than men. In addition, I also noticed that the most common way of communication is through text. 

2) I wonder why there is a disparity between the percentage of men that contact their parents compared to women? To what extent do gender roles play a role? Do men have more strained/distant relationships with their parents or is it just a gender difference as a result of socialization?

3) This relates to me and my community because I soon will be a young adult and so will my peers. During this time, we'll start to become more independent from our parents: some will move out, or will just not be as home as often because of college, work, etc. Our lives will become busier so contact might be less. It's important that we try to maintain contact though, even if we're busy because our time with them is limited.

4) Keeping Parental Contact in Adulthood
ace_sentences: Women contact their parents more often than men.
The most common way of communication is through text.
There is a disparity between the percentage of men that contact their parents and the percentage of women that contact their parents.
Gender roles may play a role in this disparity.
Men may have more strained relationships with their parents.
Men may have more distant relationships with their parents.
This disparity may be a gender difference as a result of socialization.
This relates to me and my community.
I soon will be a young adult.
My peers soon will be young adults.
During this time, we will start to become more independent from our parents.
Some people will move out.
Some people will not be as home as often because of college.
Some people will not be as home as often because of work.
Our lives will become busier.
If our lives become busier, contact might be less.
It is important that we try to maintain contact.
Even if we are busy, it is important that we try to maintain contact.
Our time with them is limited.
The title is "Keeping Parental Contact in Adulthood".
